_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 2110 batch)
_meta.note: VA LOCAL 5-pack — closing out Stafford County: SB Griffis-Widewater Chair Warner + Commonwealth's Attorney Olsen + SB Aquia first-term Regan + BoS Falmouth first-term Evans + BoS Aquia Vice Chair Guy. KEY FINDINGS: (1) Warner — Stafford SB Chair 2026 (unanimously elected Jan 13 2026, replacing Maureen Siegmund), 2nd term since Jan 2020, Griffis-Widewater 4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027. PhD biologist (UNCC) + UNC retired research scientist. D-endorsed-Independent ballot. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2 (one of 8 VA SB members who signed July 2022 amicus brief with VA Supreme Court SUPPORTING West Point Schools' firing of Peter Vlaming for refusing transgender-student pronouns: 'staff must address transgender students with the names and pronouns that reflect their gender identity'). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 (Nov 2022 publicly opposed Stafford SCPS classroom-décor restrictions that teachers/students said targeted Pride flags: 'I don't want to put a burden on our teachers or give them the impression that we're looking over their shoulders'). HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q1 (same 2022 Vlaming amicus invoked Virginia 'commonwealth's model policies for the treatment of transgender students' as compelling affirmation — aligned WITH state mandate not declined). SOFT FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2 (Jan 2022 5-2 KEEP-mask vote 'I would love to eliminate the masks as well but … in a way that is safe and thoughtful' then Feb 2022 5-2 RESCIND vote 'I do question the practicability' — late mover, restraint-skeptical net). SOFT TRUE economic_stewardship q2 (voted NO on a recent SCPS budget over $2M in new bus-driver/bus expansion and continued use of expiring ARPA pandemic-relief funds for operating expenses — rare fiscal-restraint footprint for otherwise progressive-aligned). Stafford GOP endorsed her 2023 opponent Mary Rupert (Warner 63%-36%). Religion NULL (not publicly disclosed). (2) Olsen — Stafford Commonwealth's Attorney since 2011, ran UNOPPOSED Nov 2023 (4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027), Stafford prosecutor since 1989, UMW BA + Cumberland JD, VA Prosecutor of the Year 2008, past president VA Assn of CAs, testified 2017 Senate Judiciary on youth sexual abuse. SOFT TRUE public_justice q1 (2019 stated philosophy 'tenaciously prosecuting violent and recidivist offenders' with 'diversion programs, drug court and probation for first offenders' — mainstream non-reform-prosecutor stance, anti-Soros-DA direction). Conspicuously ABSENT from May 2026 Mehaffey-led ~10-CA bloc refusing to enforce Spanberger SB 727/HB 1524 + SB 749/HB 217 — but absence from a bloc letter is NOT affirmative cited opposition, so NULL not FALSE on refuse_state_overreach q4 per cite-only discipline. Religion NULL. Institutional R prosecutor, thin movement-conservative footprint, no Mehaffey-style 2A defiance. (3) Regan — Stafford SB Aquia D-endorsed first-term, sworn Jan 2026 (4-yr term 1/2026-12/2029), succeeded Maya Guy (who took BoS seat). 19-yr public school teacher (special-ed/general-ed + head girls' basketball coach Chantilly HS Fairfax); W&M M.Ed. Educational Leadership + GMU M.Ed. SpEd + WSU BA. Wife Julia + 3 kids (2 SCPS grads + 1 in 7th grade 2025). Won 51.63%-47.72% over Annette Scharpenberg (R-endorsed) by ~302 votes. NULL across all rubric cells — deliberately narrow on-instruction-only campaign focused on teacher retention, growth/redistricting, BoS-SB relationship. Voted YES on Apr 29 2026 $525.9M FY27 SCPS budget (unanimous bipartisan, weak partisan signal so NULL not FALSE on econ q2). Voted YES on Jan 13 2026 unanimous chair election of Warner. roster_verified confidence — first-term sparse-footprint by design. Religion NULL. (4) Evans — Stafford BoS Falmouth D first-term, sworn Jan 1 2026 (4-yr term 1/2026-12/2029), succeeded Republican Meg Bohmke (who did not seek re-election; her successor Catell lost). UMGC BS Criminal Justice + MS Cybersecurity + REGENT LAW MA National Security Law (Christian institution but no personal faith profession). 22-yr criminal justice career incl 18 yrs on PUBLIC DEFENDER side representing indigent defendants; Administrative Officer Arlington CA's Office. Prior local roles Stafford Planning Commissioner + BZA Chair + SCPS SpEd Advisory Chair + NAACP Stafford Legal Redress Chair. Lost 2021 HD-88 to Phillip Scott (R) by ~16 pts. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 (YES vote 4-3 on 4/29/2026 FY27 $0.9675 rate, 4¢ increase from FY26 $0.92, cited roll call Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans YES vs Yeung+English+Vanuch NO). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 (6/3/2026 Pride proclamation 5-1 YES bloc by deduction — only English NO and Vanuch absent ill, mathematical certainty Evans was among 5 YES + 2021 HD-88 candidate self-stated 'LGBTQ+ rights' plank). Religion NULL despite Regent Law (not personal faith profession). Most Stafford context predates her tenure (12/17/2019 2A Sanctuary; 9/21/2021 anti-CRT/pronoun resolution; FY26 tax-rate vote; 6/3/2025 Pride 4-1-1; 10/22/2025 750-ft data-center setback; Kraken Loop R25-303 letter 12/2/2025) — all NULL not FALSE. (5) Guy — Stafford BoS Aquia Vice Chair D first-term BoS sworn Jan 8 2026 (4-yr term 1/2026-12/2029, succeeded Independent Monica Gary), elected Vice Chair 4-3 over Vanuch; PRIOR Stafford SB Aquia Jan 2022-Dec 2025 (one term, SB Vice Chair 2024+2025 under Chair Siegmund), succeeded on SB by Josh Regan. Self-described 'community activist' + stay-at-home mom of 6 children; full-time school/community volunteer since 2007; Education Chair Stafford NAACP; served on LOUDOUN Co Superintendent's Equity Diversity Opportunity Advisory Committee. Education not publicly stated. Endorsed by Bishop Leonard Lacey (no Guy church affiliation cited). Self-characterized 5/22/2026 'I am unbought and I am unbossed. My vote will always be my own.' HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 (YES on 4/29/2026 FY27 $0.9675 4-3 with on-dais quote 'I'm fearful of what that will do to people's — not only their livelihood — but our ability to respond to 911 calls' + 'I might be the only supervisor who would vote for the proposed rate'). HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2 (Stafford SB 2/10/2022 voted NO on rescinding universal student mask mandate + 2/22/2022 voted NO on dropping mask requirements for classroom teachers/staff/bus-drivers — 5-3 majority rescinded over her dissent both times; cited rationale 'recommendations from the medical community' = federal/state public-health deference, not refusal of overreach). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 (Stafford SB Nov 2022 brought classroom-décor revisions OUT of consent agenda for renewed debate after teachers/students framed rules as 'targeting Pride flags and other displays of support for the LGBTQ+ community' — her motion functioned to protect Pride displays in classrooms; PLUS 6/3/2026 Stafford BoS Pride proclamation 5-1 YES bloc by deduction). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2 (Stafford SB tenure: served on LOUDOUN Co Superintendent's Equity Diversity Opportunity Advisory Committee per Potomac Local 6/30/2021 + dismissed CRT criticism 'It's never even been mentioned in any of the meetings' + championed 11/18/2024 Stafford 'equitable lottery system for specialty program selection' — multi-vector equity/DEI advocacy in K-12). Religion NULL (Bishop Lacey endorsement is OF Guy not BY Guy). RICK-WEST 7TH-CONSECUTIVE-DEFER (continuing escalation): rick-west VA Mayor of Chesapeake R level=local TOP of LOCAL priority queue remains slug-collided with rick-west OK State Rep R level=state (refine-records.py line 366 `by_slug` last-wins). 7th batch to defer (0950+1047+1441+1625 on 2026-06-02 + 1550 + 2050 + 2110 on 2026-06-04). REQUEST RE-ISSUED 7X: rename VA `rick-west` to `rick-west-chesapeake`. Research COMPLETE and stashed since 2026-06-02; would score ~67/100. CROSS-BATCH STAFFORD ROSTER NOW COVERS: Vanuch+English+Diggs (prior batch 2050) + Evans+Guy (this batch) = 5-of-7 BoS evidence-finalized; Yeung+Allen remain unrefined. Stafford SB: Warner+Regan = 2-of-7 evidence-finalized; Chase+Healy+Fingerholz+Scharpenberg-replacement+Decatur-replacement remain. Stafford CA Olsen DONE. STAFFORD FY27 4-3 PARTITION CORRECTED + COMPLETE: YES Diggs+Allen+Guy+Evans (this batch confirms Evans + Guy were the 3rd + 4th YES — Allen not yet refined) vs NO Yeung+English+Vanuch. STAFFORD 2-YEAR PRIDE PATTERN: Diggs SPONSOR both years + English SOLE-NO both years + Vanuch AVOIDED-DAIS both years (away 2025 / absent ill 2026) + Guy YES both BoS-tenure year (2026) AND SB-era classroom-décor Pride-protect motion (2022) + Evans YES her BoS-tenure year (2026).

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — neither campaign site nor official Stafford SB bio nor retrievable interview states denomination or church affiliation.
profile.education: Ph.D. (interdisciplinary biology), University of North Carolina at Charlotte; B.S. biology / chemistry minor, Wingate University (NC).
profile.background: Retired research scientist (Atrium Healthcare, retired 2017); previously taught general biology / microbiology at UNCC and AP biology / chemistry at a private school. Married 42+ years, three adult children (FL, NY, Fredericksburg), two granddaughters + step-grandson — no children currently in SCPS. Civic: Potomac River environmental advocacy + mentors pre-med students. First elected Nov 2019 (sworn Jan 1 2020), defeating Jamie Decatur by ~7 pts; re-elected Nov 7 2023 over Mary Rupert 63.11%-36.37%. STAFFORD GOP ENDORSED HER OPPONENT (Rupert) in 2023; Warner was endorsed by Ben Litchfield (D, former state-senate candidate) — D-aligned independent on the non-partisan VA SB ballot. UNANIMOUSLY ELECTED BOARD CHAIR Jan 13 2026 (nominated by Sarah Chase) succeeding Maureen Siegmund; 2nd term ends Dec 31 2027.

elizabeth-warner.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Stafford SB Griffis-Widewater since Jan 2020 (re-elected Nov 2023 to 4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027), BOARD CHAIR for 2026 (unanimously elected Jan 13 2026 succeeding Maureen Siegmund). PARTY FIX: Unknown→D (D-endorsed by Litchfield in 2023; Stafford GOP endorsed her opponent Rupert; runs Independent on non-partisan VA SB ballot, scored D for endorsement-lane consistency). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null. EDUCATION: PhD biology UNCC + BS Wingate. FAMILY: married 42+ yrs, 3 adult children + grandkids, none in SCPS currently. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2 (TRANSGENDER IDEOLOGY): one of 8 VA SB members across 5 divisions who filed July 2022 AMICUS BRIEF with VA Supreme Court SUPPORTING West Point Schools' firing of teacher Peter Vlaming for refusing to use a transgender student's preferred pronouns; brief argued staff must 'address transgender students with the names and pronouns that reflect their gender identity' — directly opposite biological-sex direction. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 (LGBTQ PROMOTION IN SCHOOLS): Nov 2022 publicly opposed Stafford SCPS classroom-décor restrictions that teachers/students framed as targeting Pride flags, calling them 'vague and heavy-handed' and saying 'I don't want to put a burden on our teachers or give them the impression that we're looking over their shoulders' — defended Pride displays in classrooms against restriction. HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q1 (DECLINED STATE STRINGS): same 2022 Vlaming amicus explicitly INVOKED 'the commonwealth's model policies for the treatment of transgender students' as compelling schools to affirm gender identity — opposite direction (aligned WITH state mandate-imposing-affirmation, did NOT decline). SOFT FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2 (PROTECTED FROM LOCKDOWNS): Jan 2022 voted to MAINTAIN mask mandate in 5-2 vote ('I would love to eliminate the masks as well but … in a way that is safe and thoughtful and intentional'), then Feb 2022 voted to RESCIND in 5-2 vote ('I do question the practicability of keeping students in masks') — late-mover restraint-skeptical net signal; SOFT not HARD because she eventually voted to end. SOFT TRUE economic_stewardship q2 (FISCAL RESTRAINT): voted NO on a recent SCPS budget citing fiscal-responsibility concerns about ~$2M in new bus-driver / bus expansion AND continued use of expiring (Sept 2024) ARPA pandemic-relief funds for operating expenses — rare fiscal-restraint footprint for otherwise progressive-aligned member. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no abortion/sex-ed statement), biblical_marriage q0 (no direct marriage-definition statement; Pride displays defended in q4 instead), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT-specific vote — though Vlaming amicus is adjacent on q2-trans-pronoun-affirmation), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (no religion disclosed; no invocation/Christmas/RFRA position; Vlaming amicus aligned AGAINST teacher conscience exemption but no direct christian_liberty vote separate from biblical_marriage q2), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no SB-applicable action), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A SB tier), self_defense q0/q1 (no statement), industry_capture q0/q3 (no statement), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no specific SRO/back-the-blue/charging/diversion position located beyond classroom-décor 'thin blue line flag' descriptive mention), refuse_state_overreach q0/q3/q4 (no other subsidiarity/local-control/nullification statement located). Net 2 HARD FALSE + 1 HARD FALSE + 1 SOFT FALSE + 1 SOFT TRUE on cited cells = clean D-aligned progressive Independent profile, heterodox-light on fiscal restraint. Recommend revisiting after next FY28 budget vote + bond referendum follow-through + any first marriage/SOGI policy vote of her CHAIR tenure.

2.note: HARD: One of 8 VA SB members across 5 divisions who signed July 2022 amicus brief with VA Supreme Court SUPPORTING West Point Schools' firing of teacher Peter Vlaming for refusing transgender-student pronouns. Brief argued staff must 'address transgender students with the names and pronouns that reflect their gender identity' — opposite biological-sex-as-immutable direction.

4.note: HARD: Nov 2022 publicly opposed Stafford SCPS classroom-décor restrictions that teachers/students framed as targeting Pride flags — 'I don't want to put a burden on our teachers or give them the impression that we're looking over their shoulders' — actively defended Pride displays in classrooms against restriction. Direction: protected LGBTQ promotion in schools.

2.note: SOFT: Voted NO on SCPS budget citing fiscal-responsibility concerns about ~$2M in new bus-driver/bus expansion AND continued use of expiring (Sept 2024) ARPA pandemic-relief funds for operating expenses. Rare fiscal-restraint footprint; SOFT because single-vote, not multi-cycle pattern.

1.note: HARD: Same 2022 Vlaming amicus explicitly INVOKED 'the commonwealth's model policies for the treatment of transgender students' as the legal basis compelling schools to affirm gender identity — aligned WITH state mandate-imposing-affirmation, opposite of declining state strings that compel ungodly local policy.

2.note: SOFT: Jan 2022 voted to MAINTAIN mask mandate in 5-2 vote ('I would love to eliminate the masks as well but … in a way that is safe and thoughtful'), then Feb 2022 voted to RESCIND 5-2 ('I do question the practicability of keeping students in masks') — late-mover net signal on COVID-mandate restraint; SOFT because she did eventually vote to end.

profile.education: B.A. University of Mary Washington; J.D. Samford University (Cumberland School of Law).
profile.background: Stafford prosecutor since 1989 (line ADA prior to election as Commonwealth's Attorney). Elected CA 2011; re-elected 2015, 2019, 2023 (ran unopposed in 2023). Virginia Prosecutor of the Year 2008. Past President, Virginia Association of Commonwealth's Attorneys; former Vice President, National District Attorneys Association. Testified before U.S. Senate Judiciary Committee 2017 (USA Gymnastics / Nassar hearings on youth sexual abuse). Lives in Stafford with wife. Current 4-yr term Jan 2024 – Dec 31 2027.

eric-olsen.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Stafford Commonwealth's Attorney since 2011, ran unopposed Nov 2023 (4-yr term 1/2024-12/2027); Stafford prosecutor since 1989 (35-year career), Cumberland JD, UMW BA. VA Prosecutor of the Year 2008; past president VA Assn of CAs; testified 2017 Senate Judiciary on youth sexual abuse. Stafford GOP roster lists him at 'Team Member: Commonwealth's Attorney.' STILL SEATED as of 2026-06-04 (issued public charging statement on I-95 bus crash 5/30/2026). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null. SOFT TRUE public_justice q1 (NON-ROGUE-PROSECUTOR DIRECTION): 2019 stated philosophy 'tenaciously prosecuting violent and recidivist offenders' while using 'diversion programs, drug court and probation for first offenders' — mainstream non-reform-prosecutor stance, anti-Soros-DA direction. SOFT not HARD because single-cycle stated philosophy, no multi-vector recent reaffirmation. CONSPICUOUSLY ABSENT from May 2026 Mehaffey-led ~10-CA VA bloc refusing to enforce Spanberger SB 727/HB 1524 (public-carry ban) + SB 749/HB 217 (assault-weapons + 15-rd magazine ban) — bloc enumeration in WTVR + Virginia Scope + WJLA names Spotsylvania (Mehaffey), Warren (Bell), Pulaski, Powhatan, Smyth, Goochland, Scott; Olsen on NONE of them despite being next-door R CA in I-95 corridor. ABSENCE FROM BLOC IS NOT AFFIRMATIVE CITED OPPOSITION → NULL not FALSE on refuse_state_overreach q4 per cite-only discipline; flag as a meaningful negative datum noted in narrative but unscored. NO joint-LE press conference with Sheriff Decatur located; no charging-policy memo published; no declination data; no asset-forfeiture position; no Mark Taylor / Shamgar Connors-equivalent local Christian-liberty prosecutorial action; no 287(g)-versus-Decatur statement; no abortion / Dobbs-implementation policy; no Pride / SOGI / trans-prisoner policy. The 5/30/2026 same-day I-95 bus-crash felony charging announcement ('probable cause presently exists to establish that the driver of the tour bus caused this crash and, at the time of the crash, he was driving in a criminally negligent manner') is routine institutional CA function NOT affirmative pro-LE advocacy, so does NOT clear public_justice q0 high-bar (Mehaffey's q0 was cleared by an EXPLICIT joint-coordination letter to his sheriff, not by charging announcements alone). NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1, biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4, family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2, christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (religion not disclosed), economic_stewardship q2, election_integrity q0/q1/q4, border_immigration q1/q2 (Decatur sets detainer policy, Olsen silent), self_defense q0/q1, industry_capture q0/q3, public_justice q0/q2/q3/q4, refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2/q3/q4. Net 1 SOFT TRUE on cited cells; thin institutional-R career-prosecutor footprint, NOT a movement-conservative Mehaffey-style CA. Recommend revisiting if he publishes a charging-policy memo, releases declination data, or weighs in on any Spanberger 2A laws / sanctuary / Mark Taylor / Shamgar Connors-equivalent prosecutorial decision.

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — neither campaign site nor official Stafford SB bio states denomination or church affiliation.
profile.education: B.A. Washington State University; M.Ed. Special Education, George Mason University; M.Ed. Educational Leadership, William & Mary.
profile.background: 19-year public school teacher, currently special-education / general-education teacher AND head varsity girls' basketball coach at Chantilly High School (Fairfax County). Previously 4 years at Colonial Forge HS and 7 years at Brooke Point HS (Stafford). Married to Julia; three children — two graduated from Stafford schools, one entered 7th grade in 2025. Aquia district resident 14 years. First-time SB candidate. Won Nov 4 2025 Stafford SB Aquia District race 51.63% (4,319) vs Annette Scharpenberg (R-endorsed) 47.72% (4,017) by ~302 votes. Endorsed by Stafford County Democratic Committee. Opponent endorsed by Stafford GOP. Sworn Jan 2026; succeeded Maya Guy (D, who moved up to BoS Aquia Vice Chair). 4-yr term ends Dec 31 2029.

josh-regan.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Stafford SB Aquia D-endorsed first-term, sworn Jan 2026 (4-yr term 1/2026-12/2029), succeeded Maya Guy (who took BoS Aquia seat). PARTY FIX: Unknown→D (Stafford Dems endorsed; opponent Scharpenberg endorsed by Stafford GOP; runs Independent on non-partisan VA SB ballot, scored D for endorsement-lane consistency). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null. EDUCATION: WSU BA + GMU M.Ed. SpEd + W&M M.Ed. Educational Leadership. PROFESSION: 19-yr public school teacher, currently SpEd + girls' basketball head coach at Chantilly HS Fairfax. FAMILY: wife Julia + 3 kids (2 SCPS grads + 1 in 7th grade). Won 51.63%-47.72% over Scharpenberg by ~302 votes. NULL ACROSS ALL RUBRIC CELLS — deliberately narrow on-instruction-only campaign focused on teacher retention, growth/redistricting, and BoS-SB relationship; opponent owned the conservative-contrast issues (Scharpenberg made library content + bathrooms-by-biological-sex + sports central, Regan declined to engage). Voted YES on 4/29/2026 unanimous $525.9M FY27 SCPS budget (4.75% raise + $54K starting salary + $10.6M new-school operating) — but unanimous bipartisan-consent budget, weak partisan signal → NULL not FALSE on economic_stewardship q2 per cite-only discipline (Vanuch et al on BoS-side also voted yes on related items; not a partisan-distinguishing vote). Voted YES on 1/13/2026 unanimous chair election of Warner. His 8/18/2025 'School Board Responsibilities' blog explicitly pledges 'I won't waste our (or your) valuable time on things we can't change' and 'I won't pursue policies that run counter to established Virginia law' — pro-rule-of-law / pro-VA-state-law deference, but no specific subsidiarity-violation cited so NULL not FALSE on refuse_state_overreach. NOT FOUND iVoterGuide profile (he does not appear in ivoterguide.com search); no Moms for Liberty / Rappahannock Conservative Women's Coalition affiliation (likely NO given D endorsement); not on board during 2022 Vlaming amicus (not yet on any school board then); not on board during Jan/Feb 2022 mask votes; silent on transgender / SOGI / Title IX / library content (Gender Queer / All Boys Aren't Blue); silent on school choice / ESA; silent on CRT / 1619; silent on prayer / invocation / religious clubs / Mark Taylor lawsuit; silent on 2A; silent on ICE / 287(g). NOT scored across all 30 LOCAL-applicable cells. roster_verified confidence — first-term sparse-footprint by design, only ~5 months on board. Recommend revisiting after first full SB cycle of post-budget votes (FY28 + any library / transgender / parental-rights policy vote of his first chair tenure).

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Falmouth District

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND of stated denomination or church affiliation. Holds Regent University School of Law degree (Christian institution) but Regent attendance is not a personal faith profession — many Regent alumni do not identify as Christian-conservative.
profile.education: B.S. Criminal Justice; M.S. Cybersecurity and Digital Forensics, University of Maryland Global College; M.A. in Law (National Security Law concentration), Regent University School of Law. Garfield Senior HS (NJ) 1996.
profile.background: Born Long Branch NJ, raised military dependent; Stafford resident since 2015. Wife of one, mother of four. 22+ years criminal justice career, including 18 years on the PUBLIC DEFENDER side representing indigent defendants; currently Administrative Officer with Arlington County Commonwealth's Attorney's Office. Prior Stafford civic: Planning Commissioner; Chair/Secretary Board of Zoning Appeals; Chair Stafford SCPS Special Education Advisory Committee; Chair Parks & Rec Commission; Legal Redress Chair, NAACP Stafford Branch. Lost 2021 VA HD-88 to Phillip Scott (R) by ~16 pts. Won Nov 4 2025 Stafford BoS Falmouth race 4,815 vs Michael Catell (R) 4,193, succeeding retiring R Meg Bohmke (Catell was Bohmke's hand-picked successor). Sworn Jan 1 2026 alongside Maya Guy as first Black-majority Stafford BoS in county's 362-year history; 4-yr term ends Dec 31 2029. Endorsed by VA Democratic Women Caucus, IBEW Local 26, NoVa Labor, The Collective PAC, Mid-Atlantic Pipe Trades, Mountain View PAC of Educators, FAAR; political endorsers include Del. Joshua Cole, Sen. Jennifer Carroll Foy, Joel Griffin, Andrea Bailey, Margaret Franklin, Elizabeth Guzman.

profile.background: Self-described 'community activist' and stay-at-home mother of six children. No paid employment cited; full-time school/community volunteer since 2007. Education Chair, Stafford NAACP. PTA roles at Brooke Point HS, Moncure Elementary, Shirley Heim MS. Served on LOUDOUN County Superintendent's Equity, Diversity & Opportunity Advisory Committee. PRIOR SB SERVICE: Stafford SB Aquia, Jan 2022 – Dec 2025 (one term); served as SB Vice Chair 2024 + 2025 (under Chair Maureen Siegmund); succeeded on SB by Josh Regan (D-endorsed). Won Nov 4 2025 Stafford BoS Aquia race 4,912 (~58.2%) vs Hank Scharpenberg (R) 3,524 (~41.8%) in three-way contest including former IND Monica Gary. Sworn BoS Jan 8 2026 alongside Kecia Evans as first Black-majority Stafford BoS in county's 362-year history. Elected Vice Chair Jan 2026, 4-3 over Crystal Vanuch (party-line). 4-yr term ends Dec 31 2029. Self-characterized 5/22/2026 BoS meeting: 'I am unbought and I am unbossed. My vote will always be my own.'

maya-guy.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Stafford BoS Aquia VICE CHAIR D, sworn BoS Jan 8 2026 (4-yr term 1/2026-12/2029, succeeded Independent Monica Gary), elected VC Jan 2026 4-3 over Vanuch; PRIOR Stafford SB Aquia Jan 2022 – Dec 2025 (one term), SB Vice Chair 2024+2025 under Chair Siegmund, succeeded on SB by Josh Regan. PARTY confirmed D. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null (Bishop Lacey endorsement is OF Guy, not BY Guy of any faith identity). EDUCATION: not publicly stated anywhere. PROFESSION: stay-at-home mother of 6 + full-time community volunteer since 2007. NAACP Stafford Education Chair + Loudoun Superintendent's Equity Diversity Opportunity Advisory Committee. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 (FISCAL/BUDGET RESTRAINT): YES on 4/29/2026 FY27 $0.9675 real-estate tax rate 4-3 (4¢ hike from FY26 $0.92), cited roll YES Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans vs NO Yeung+English+Vanuch — and on-dais cited quote 'I'm fearful of what that will do to people's — not only their livelihood — but our ability to respond to 911 calls' + 'I might be the only supervisor who would vote for the proposed rate' (Potomac Local 4/29/2026 + Fredericksburg Free Press 4/30/2026 + FXBG Advance). HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q2 (PROTECTED FROM LOCKDOWNS/MANDATES): Stafford SB 2/10/2022 voted NO on rescinding universal student mask mandate + 2/22/2022 voted NO on dropping mask requirements for classroom teachers/staff/bus-drivers — 5-3 majority rescinded over her dissent BOTH TIMES; cited rationale 'recommendations from the medical community' = federal/state public-health deference, OPPOSITE of refusing state-overreach lockdown direction. Multi-vote pattern across 2 weeks, clears HARD bar. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4 (LGBTQ PROMOTION IN SCHOOLS + PUBLIC POLICY): (a) Stafford SB Nov 2022 brought classroom-décor revisions OUT of consent agenda for renewed debate after teachers/students framed rules as 'targeting Pride flags and other displays of support for the LGBTQ+ community' — her motion functioned to protect Pride displays in classrooms (fredericksburg.com 11/16/2022); (b) 6/3/2026 Stafford BoS Pride proclamation 5-1 YES bloc by mathematical deduction (English sole NO, Vanuch absent ill — leaves Guy+Diggs+Evans+Allen+Yeung as the 5 YES). Multi-vector cross-tenure (SB-era + BoS-era) HARD. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2 (OPPOSE CRT/SOGI IN K-12): (a) served on LOUDOUN County Superintendent's Equity, Diversity & Opportunity Advisory Committee (Potomac Local 6/30/2021); (b) dismissed CRT criticism on Stafford SB: 'It's never even been mentioned in any of the meetings ... We've never mentioned it'; (c) championed Nov 2024 Stafford 'equitable lottery system for specialty program selection' replacing prior selection process — multi-vector equity/DEI advocacy in K-12, clears HARD bar. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no abortion/IVF statement), biblical_marriage q0/q2 (no direct marriage-definition or trans-ideology vote located independent of q4 evidence), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1 (no school-choice or parental-notification specific vote), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (no invocation/RFRA/Christmas/Mark-Taylor statement; religion not disclosed), economic_stewardship q0 (no specific spending-discipline single vote separate from FY27 tax rate q2), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no SB-applicable or BoS-applicable action), border_immigration q1/q2 (no 287(g) statement; Sheriff Decatur rejected 287(g) and Guy silent), self_defense q0/q1 (12/17/2019 2A Sanctuary predates her tenure; no personal 2A statement located), industry_capture q0/q3 (no statement), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (her tax-hike rationale cited 911 response is funding-side NOT affirmative pro-LE advocacy — runbook explicitly says 'voting a budget that funds police' does NOT clear high-bar — null not TRUE; no defund/bail/back-the-blue vote of her tenure located either direction), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q3/q4 (no nullification/declining-strings/subsidiarity statement located beyond q2 mask pattern). Voted YES Jan 8 2026 organizational meeting on Diggs Chair 5-2 (presumed but not in cited source) and was elected VC herself 4-3 over Vanuch (party-line). Voted NO on Buc-ee's 5-2 (5/20/2026, with Yeung; followed by 5/22/2026 'unbought, unbossed' dais quote). Net 4 HARD FALSE on cited cells across both SB-era and BoS-era; strong textbook progressive-D profile with multi-vector equity/SOGI/COVID-mandate/tax-and-spend record. Existing scorecard 'sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE' + 'border_immigration q0 TRUE' + 'self_defense q0-q4 FALSE' marks were unsupported guesses — reset_unspecified will clear them. Recommend revisiting after first marriage/abortion/2A-specific vote of her VC tenure.

4.note: HARD multi-vector cross-tenure: (a) Stafford SB Nov 2022 motion to bring classroom-décor revisions OUT of consent agenda for renewed debate after public comment framed restrictions as targeting Pride flags — her motion functioned to protect Pride displays in classrooms; (b) 6/3/2026 Stafford BoS Pride proclamation 5-1 YES bloc by mathematical deduction (English sole NO, Vanuch absent ill — leaves Guy+Diggs+Evans+Allen+Yeung as the 5 YES). Multi-cycle SB-era + BoS-era promotion-direction record.

2.note: HARD multi-vector: (a) served on LOUDOUN County Superintendent's Equity, Diversity & Opportunity Advisory Committee (per Potomac Local 6/30/2021); (b) on Stafford SB dismissed CRT criticism 'It's never even been mentioned in any of the meetings... We've never mentioned it'; (c) championed Nov 2024 Stafford 'equitable lottery system for specialty program selection' — multi-vector equity/DEI advocacy in K-12.

HARD: YES on 4/29/2026 FY27 $0.9675 real-estate tax rate (4¢ hike from FY26 $0.92), 4-3 cited roll YES Diggs+Guy+Allen+Evans vs NO Yeung+English+Vanuch; on-dais cited quote 'I'm fearful of what that will do to people's — not only their livelihood — but our ability to respond to 911 calls' + 'I might be the only supervisor who would vote for the proposed rate' — explicit advocacy for the increase.

HARD multi-vote pattern: Stafford SB 2/10/2022 voted NO on rescinding universal student mask mandate AND 2/22/2022 voted NO on dropping mask requirements for classroom teachers/staff/bus-drivers — 5-3 majority rescinded over her dissent both times. Cited rationale 'recommendations from the medical community' = state public-health deference, opposite of refusing state-overreach lockdown direction.
